name: Aether (mythology)
description: In Greek mythology, Aether, Æther, Aither, or Ether (/ˈiː.θər/; Ancient Greek: Αἰθήρ, .mw-parser-output .IPA-label-small{font-size:85%}.mw-parser-output .references .IPA-label-small,.mw-parser-output .infobox .IPA-label-small,.mw-parser-output .navbox .IPA-label-small{font-size:100%}pronounced [ai̯tʰɛ̌ːr], meaning "brightness"[2]) is the personification of the bright upper sky. According to Hesiod, he was the son of Erebus (Darkness) and Nyx (Night), and the brother of Hemera (Day).[3] In Orphic cosmogony, Aether was the offspring of Chronos (Time) and the brother of Ch
